titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the New Haven Miscellany
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 16090312.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 2, 2022 at 03:52

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection encompasses 19th- and early 20th-century accessions of manuscripts pertaining to New Haven, Connecticut, which the library chose to group and list as an artificial collection. Papers relate to financial, political, religious, and social matters from 1702 to 1889. Among those represented in the collection are the Atwater family, Isaac Beers, David Daggett, Joseph Darling, Samuel J. Hitchcock, Nathaniel Jocelyn, Nathaniel Lyon, Roger Sherman, Seth Perkins Staples, Charles Tomlinson, and Chauncey Whittelsey.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by individual, organization, or category.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: New Haven Miscellany. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Materials in this collection were grouped together by the Library in the late 19th or early 20th century.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: Atwater Family Deeds (GEN MSS 1912), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.
Associated material: Lyon Family Business Papers (GEN MSS 1960), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.
Associated material: Samuel J. Hitchcock Papers (GEN MSS 1878), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection encompasses 19th- and early 20th-century accessions of manuscripts pertaining to New Haven, Connecticut, which the library chose to group and list as an artificial collection. Papers relate to financial, political, religious, and social matters from 1702 to 1889. Among those represented in the collection are the Atwater family, Isaac Beers, David Daggett, Joseph Darling, Samuel J. Hitchcock, Nathaniel Jocelyn, Nathaniel Lyon, Roger Sherman, Seth Perkins Staples, Charles Tomlinson, and Chauncey Whittelsey.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The New Haven Miscellany is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A narration of a religious experience read before admission to the First Church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deed to his son Enos Atwater

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deed to his son Elihu Atwater

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Account for leveling the Green and other papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft form and certificate of exemption for a drafted person on account of disability

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signature to deed of sale of three acres to Nathaniel Bradley

did.unittitle: New Haven Society of Architectural and Rural Improvement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A table of fees established by the Attornies practising at the Bar in N.H. County"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to his role as postmaster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Loose diary pages with copies of letters from his grandfather Eli Ives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bill to "Mr. Patterson" for board and washing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Quitclaim deed to Reverend Joseph Noyes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deed to his son Warham Williams
